title: Non-squeezing and other rigidity results in LCS geometry
speaker: Pranav Chakravarthy
abstract: Locally conformally symplectic (LCS) manifolds are generalisations of symplectic manifolds where the 2-form is not closed but instead satisfies the identity dω = η ∧ ω for a closed 1-form η. The study of these manifolds is equivalent to that of symplectic manifolds when η is exact; however, they resemble the behaviour of contact manifolds when η has no zeroes. Using the theory of generating functions for Lagrangians in the twisted cotangent bundle, we define spectral selectors for Hamiltonian LCS diffeomorphisms of S^1 X R^2n X S^1 and S^1 X R^(2n+1) and a LCS capacity for domains in S^1 X R^2n X S^1, thereby giving us a version of the non-squeezing theorem on this manifold. Time permitting, we shall also see how we can define a partial order on the group of compactly supported LCS Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms on S^1 X R^2n X S^1 and S^1 X R^(2n+1) and a bi-invariant metric on the group of compactly supported LCS Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms of S^1 X R^2n X S^1. This is joint work with Mélanie Bertelson and Margherita Sandon.

proof_stages.label: LCS definitions, equivalences, covers, and products
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines locally conformally symplectic manifolds via a nondegenerate two-form and closed Lee form satisfying the twisted-closed equation, then introduces conformal equivalence, the symplectic cover, and the LCS product needed for graph constructions.

proof_stages.label: Lee dynamics as the replacement for holomorphic-curve tools
proof_stages.summary: After explaining why ordinary holomorphic-curve compactness and Lagrangian rigidity are weak in LCS geometry, the speaker defines the Lee vector field, Lee flow, Lee chords, their actions, and the essential-chord condition as the Reeb-like structure to use instead.

proof_stages.label: Generating functions for exact LCS Lagrangians
proof_stages.summary: Using the twisted cotangent and twisted jet-bundle formalism, exact Lagrangians Hamiltonian-isotopic to the zero section acquire generating functions, and critical points of these functions are identified with essential Lee chords, yielding cup-length and Betti-number lower bounds.

proof_stages.label: Translated points and local graph reduction
proof_stages.summary: Translated points of LCS Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms are defined and, for C0-small maps, the graph in the LCS product is locally identified with a twisted cotangent neighborhood so that essential translated points are counted by the preceding generating-function theory.

proof_stages.label: Global model-space generating functions and the nonsqueezing target
proof_stages.summary: For model conformal symplectizations such as S1 times R^{2n} times S1, a strict LCS identification of the product with a twisted cotangent bundle gives generating functions for all compactly supported Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms, leading to the stated Hamiltonian nonsqueezing problem for LCS balls.

proof_stages.label: Spectral selectors and LCS capacity
proof_stages.summary: Quadratic-at-infinity generating functions define spectral selectors c+ and c-, whose integer parts have the required conjugation behavior; taking suprema over compactly supported Hamiltonian maps defines an LCS capacity related to contact and Viterbo capacities.

proof_stages.label: Capacity obstruction to Hamiltonian squeezing
proof_stages.summary: Assuming a Hamiltonian LCS squeezing map would force, by invariance and monotonicity, incompatible integer-valued capacities for the larger and smaller balls whenever their pi r squared values are separated by an integer, proving the nonsqueezing theorem.

proof_stages.label: Spectral metric and orderability consequences
proof_stages.summary: The same spectral selectors define an integer-valued bi-invariant metric and a spectral partial order on Hamiltonian LCS diffeomorphisms, imply orderability results for the model spaces and twisted cotangent bundles, and leave open how LCS orderability should control squeezing.

examples.note: Setting the Lee form to zero recovers ordinary symplectic manifolds, making the LCS definition concrete without being used as a proof step.

examples.engagement_reason: The speaker checks what Lee chords and their actions become in the symplectic, conformal symplectization, and twisted cotangent cases.

examples.note: The known contact squeezing of balls of radius below one lifts to the LCS setting, illustrating the limitation and sharpness context of the nonsqueezing statement.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker explains that the subunit-radius contact squeezing map can be lifted to the LCS manifold to produce the corresponding LCS squeezing.
